title: GPT-5.5 rolls out to ChatGPT paid users
summary: OpenAI began a gradual rollout of GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise ChatGPT subscribers today. The new Thinking variant is pitched as the most capable reasoning model in ChatGPT, able to handle complex multi-step tasks, while API access will follow later with additional safeguards in place.

title: OpenAI splits ChatGPT Pro into $100 and $200 tiers
summary: OpenAI restructured its Pro offering into two tiers: $100/month for 5x usage versus Plus, and $200/month for 20x usage, both including access to GPT-5.5 Pro, Codex, and Deep Research. The $100 tier launches with a temporary 10x Codex bonus through May 31 to sweeten the deal.

title: ComfyUI hits $500M valuation after $30M raise
summary: ComfyUI, the open-source node-based tool that gives creators granular control over AI image, video, and audio generation, closed a $30 million funding round at a $500 million valuation. The raise reflects growing demand from creators who want more hands-on control than turnkey AI apps provide.

title: Replicate adds Moonshot Kimi-K2 and Alibaba Happy Horse
summary: Replicate's official model catalog expanded this week with Moonshot AI's Kimi-K2, a one-trillion-parameter open model built for long-horizon coding and agent swarms with a 262k context window. Alibaba's Happy Horse 1.0 also landed, offering text-to-video and image-to-video generation at 720p and 1080p.

title: DeepMind's David Silver raises $1.1B for data-free AI lab
summary: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded just months ago by AlphaGo creator David Silver, secured $1.1 billion at a $5.1 billion valuation. The lab is pursuing AI systems that learn without human-labeled data, a fundamental departure from how most frontier models are trained today.

title: Google commits up to $40B to Anthropic
summary: Google announced plans to invest up to $40 billion in Anthropic in a combination of cash and compute, cementing its position as the startup's largest backer. The deal follows the limited release of Anthropic's cybersecurity-focused Mythos model and comes as Anthropic's annualized revenue reportedly surged past $30 billion.

title: OpenAI resolves Microsoft conflict over AWS deal
summary: OpenAI has secured major concessions from Microsoft that clear the path for it to sell products on Amazon Web Services, ending a legal dispute between the two companies. In exchange, Microsoft receives an expanded revenue-share agreement, signaling a careful rebalancing of one of AI's most important partnerships.

title: Sierra acquires YC-backed Fragment
summary: Sierra, the AI customer service agent company co-founded by Bret Taylor, has acquired Fragment, a French YC-backed startup. The acquisition is expected to deepen Sierra's capabilities as demand for enterprise-grade AI agents accelerates across industries.

title: 120,000 authors chase share of Anthropic's $1.5B copyright settlement
summary: Nearly 120,000 authors and copyright holders have filed to claim a portion of a $1.5 billion class-action settlement with Anthropic over unauthorized use of their books in AI training data. The settlement is among the largest copyright resolutions in the AI industry and could set precedent for future training-data disputes.

title: Musk vs. Altman trial kicks off with $150B on the line
summary: Jury selection began Monday in San Francisco for Elon Musk's lawsuit against Sam Altman and OpenAI, with opening arguments set for Tuesday. Musk claims OpenAI betrayed its nonprofit charter when it pivoted toward profit, and he is seeking up to $150 billion in damages for its charitable arm — a verdict that could reshape AI governance and nonprofit law.

title: 600 Google employees demand CEO reject classified Pentagon AI deal
summary: More than 600 Google staff signed an open letter urging CEO Sundar Pichai to turn down a proposed Defense Department contract that would extend Gemini's capabilities into classified military operations. Google already holds a non-classified AI contract with the DoD, making this a direct escalation of the long-running employee activism over military AI.

title: US judges scramble to set AI policies for their chambers
summary: Federal judges across the country are grappling with how AI tools should — and should not — be used in court filings and legal research, with policies ranging from outright bans to cautious endorsements. The patchwork approach reflects broader uncertainty about AI reliability after several high-profile incidents of AI-generated errors in legal documents.

title: BXAI-OS listed in NIST catalog ahead of Colorado AI deadline
summary: BXAI-OS has been officially cataloged as a NIST Informative Reference, providing organizations a machine-executable framework for AI governance compliance ahead of Colorado's June 30, 2026 SB 24-205 deadline. The listing arrives as California and New York move toward their own AI regulations, tightening a growing patchwork of state-level AI rules.

title: GitHub Copilot adds Claude and Codex to Agent HQ
summary: GitHub's Agent HQ now lets developers pick between Anthropic's Claude and OpenAI's Codex directly inside GitHub and VS Code. The feature is in public preview for Copilot Pro+ and Enterprise subscribers, expanding the platform's multi-agent orchestration ambitions first unveiled at Universe 2025.

title: GitHub Copilot SDK opens to all developers
summary: A new GitHub Copilot SDK is now in technical preview, letting any application embed a programmable agent layer that can plan tasks, invoke tools, edit files, and run commands. GitHub is positioning this as a way to bake AI-native workflows into third-party apps, not just its own surfaces.

title: OpenAI restructures ChatGPT Pro into two tiers
summary: OpenAI has split its $200 ChatGPT Pro plan, adding a new $100 mid-tier that offers 5x usage limits over Plus and 10x Codex access through May 31. The restructure reflects growing demand for Codex and Deep Research among professional users who don't need the full unlimited-tier firepower.

title: Google donates Agent Payments Protocol to FIDO Alliance
summary: Google open-sourced version 0.2 of its Agent Payments Protocol (AP2) on GitHub and handed stewardship to the FIDO Alliance. The update introduces 'Human Not Present' payment flows, allowing AI agents to autonomously complete pre-authorized purchases — like snagging concert tickets the instant they go on sale.

title: FLUX.2 family brings sub-second image gen to production
summary: Black Forest Labs' FLUX.2 lineup on Replicate now spans three tiers: Max for ultra-high-fidelity product photography, Pro for high-volume editing with structured JSON prompting, and Klein 4B — a 4-billion-parameter distilled model capable of sub-second generation for real-time apps. All tiers support multi-reference image blending.

title: DeepMind co-founder raises $1.1B for Ineffable Intelligence
summary: David Silver, the DeepMind researcher behind AlphaGo, has secured $1.1 billion for his new British AI lab Ineffable Intelligence at a $5.1 billion valuation. The startup is pursuing AI systems that learn without human-labeled data, betting on reinforcement learning as the path to more autonomous AI.

title: Tencent and Alibaba circle DeepSeek at $20B+ valuation
summary: Chinese tech giants Tencent and Alibaba are in advanced talks to invest in DeepSeek, the AI lab owned by hedge fund High-Flyer Capital Management, at a valuation exceeding $20 billion. It would be DeepSeek's first external fundraise, underscoring how seriously Chinese incumbents are taking the upstart's rapid rise.

title: Space defense startup True Anomaly lands $650M Series D
summary: True Anomaly, which builds autonomous spacecraft and software for U.S. national security missions, closed a $650 million Series D that lifts its total funding to $1 billion. The round signals sustained investor appetite for AI-driven defense technology as geopolitical tensions around space assets intensify.

title: Counterpart raises $96M to insure AI-related damage
summary: Insurtech startup Counterpart closed a $96 million round, bringing total capital to $106 million, to expand its AI-powered liability insurance products. The raise comes as enterprises face mounting legal exposure from AI errors, bias claims, and regulatory penalties.

title: Windmill pulls $12M to reinvent AI-era performance reviews
summary: HR tech startup Windmill raised $12 million across pre-seed and seed rounds to build fairer, more frequent performance evaluations using AI. The founders argue that as AI-driven workplace anxiety grows, companies need objective and auditable feedback systems that reduce managerial bias.

title: Musk vs. Altman trial kicks off in California
summary: Jury selection began Monday in San Francisco for Elon Musk's lawsuit against Sam Altman and OpenAI, with opening arguments set for Tuesday. Musk is seeking up to $150 billion in damages, alleging OpenAI betrayed its nonprofit charter when it pivoted toward a for-profit structure — a case that could redefine how AI companies are legally obligated to honor their founding missions.

title: DOJ backs xAI lawsuit to kill Colorado's AI Act
summary: The Department of Justice intervened on April 24 in xAI's lawsuit challenging Colorado's sweeping AI Act, marking the first time the federal government has moved to invalidate a state AI law under Trump's Executive Order 14365. The DOJ argued the law violates the Equal Protection Clause by compelling AI companies to factor demographic characteristics into model design, framing state-level AI regulation as a barrier to national competitiveness.

title: China blocks Meta's $2B acquisition of Manus
summary: Beijing formally blocked Meta's $2 billion bid to acquire Manus, an AI agent startup, following a months-long regulatory probe. The decision underscores how cross-border AI acquisitions are increasingly caught in geopolitical crossfire, with data sovereignty and AI agent capabilities now treated as strategic national interests.

title: OpenAI ends Microsoft exclusivity, expands to AWS
summary: OpenAI has resolved its long-running legal and commercial tensions with Microsoft, clearing the way to offer its models on Amazon Web Services and potentially other cloud providers. The deal marks a significant shift from the exclusive Azure partnership that defined OpenAI's commercial infrastructure for years.

title: AI's regulatory honeymoon is officially over in 2026
summary: A wave of major AI regulations is hitting simultaneously: the EU AI Act's high-risk provisions take full effect in August with penalties up to €35 million, Colorado's AI Act is due June 30, California's AI Transparency Act kicks in by August, and a 42-state attorney general coalition is ramping up coordinated enforcement. Companies that treated compliance as optional are now facing a hard reckoning.

title: Gemini remembers you — and will steal your ChatGPT history
summary: Google is rolling out a Memories feature to UK Gemini users that lets the assistant learn preferences across conversations over time. More aggressively, it now lets users import their full chat history and saved memories from rival AI apps via ZIP file or a copy-paste prompt flow. The move is a direct shot at user lock-in across the AI assistant market.

title: Kiri.ai drops a 1-trillion-parameter open model on Replicate
summary: Moonshot AI's Kimi model — 1 trillion parameters, 262k context window, with vision and tool use — is now available via Replicate's API alongside a wave of other frontier models. The Replicate catalog now spans everything from OpenAI's GPT image generation to Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 and ByteDance's multimodal video model. The breadth signals that production-ready model access is becoming a commodity.

title: FLUX 2 Max targets product photography and character consistency
summary: Black Forest Labs' FLUX 2 family now covers the full spectrum from sub-second prototyping with FLUX 2 Klein to premium fidelity with FLUX 2 Max, which supports up to 8 reference images for character and product consistency. Structured JSON prompting and precise hex color control make it a serious contender for commercial creative workflows. The family is available production-ready via Replicate.

title: Google Translate turns 20 with Gemini-powered idiom smarts
summary: Google marked the 20th anniversary of Translate by integrating Gemini models into its Live interpretation feature, which now handles slang, idioms, and tonal nuance rather than just literal translation. Earbuds can now act as real-time interpreters that preserve a speaker's rhythm and register. The upgrade reflects how Gemini is quietly becoming the engine behind Google's entire product surface.

title: FIDO Alliance sets safety rules for AI agents buying things online
summary: The FIDO Alliance announced new authentication standards specifically designed for AI agents that shop, log in, and transact on users' behalf. The initiative addresses a fast-growing identity problem: as agents proliferate, most organizations have no controls over which agents access which data or accounts. The standards aim to prevent agents from going rogue with payment credentials before the problem becomes widespread.

title: Scout AI raises $100M to train models for battlefield ATVs
summary: Scout AI, a self-described frontier lab for defense founded in 2024, closed a $100M Series A led by Align Ventures and Draper Associates. The startup builds autonomous military all-terrain vehicles and trains the AI models that operate them. It previously raised a $15M seed in January 2025, making this a rapid scale-up in a sector attracting serious venture capital.

title: Aidoc pulls in $150M Series E for clinical AI imaging
summary: Clinical AI provider Aidoc secured $150M in a Series E led by Goldman Sachs Alternatives, with General Catalyst, SoftBank, and NVIDIA's NVentures also participating. The company, which applies AI to medical imaging workflows, has now raised $520M in total. The round comes weeks after rival RadAI closed a $60M Series C at a $525M valuation, signaling fierce competition in radiology AI.

title: BMW i Ventures launches $300M fund with AI and robotics thesis
summary: BMW's independent venture arm announced a $300M third fund focused on agentic AI, physical AI, robotics, and industrial software for the automotive sector. The new fund brings BMW i Ventures to $1.1B in total capital under management and will invest from early stage through Series B in North America and Europe. No investments from the new fund have been announced yet.

title: Pursuit lands $22M seed to help companies win government contracts
summary: Pursuit, a platform that helps businesses find and win government contracts, announced a $22M seed round led by OpenGov co-founder Mike Rosengarten, with backing from Bill Gurley, Jack Altman, and Sam Hinkie. The company launched in 2023 out of a simple observation: selling to government is surprisingly hard for most companies. Total funding now stands at $25.5M.

title: Shapes raises $8M for AI characters in your group chats
summary: Shapes, an app that embeds AI characters directly into human group conversations, emerged from stealth with $8M in seed funding led by Lightspeed. Think Discord, but where AI personas are full participants in the chat alongside real users. The company plans to use the capital to accelerate user acquisition in what is shaping up to be a crowded social AI space.

title: EU AI Act talks collapse after marathon session
summary: After 12 hours of negotiations, EU member states and European Parliament lawmakers failed to agree on amendments to the AI Act that would have delayed high-risk compliance deadlines from August 2026 to 2027 or later. The core sticking point is whether AI embedded in already-regulated products like medical devices and toys should be exempt from additional AI Act requirements. If talks stall through May and June, companies that built their timelines around the extension could face immediate compliance obligations they are not ready for.

title: Google signed a classified AI deal with the Pentagon — quietly
summary: Google reportedly agreed to let the US Department of Defense use its AI models for any lawful government purpose under a classified contract, placing it alongside OpenAI and xAI in the set of companies with secret military AI agreements. The deal reportedly bars the company from blocking lawful government uses and requires it to help adjust safety filters when asked. The contract was finalized less than a day after Google employees publicly asked Sundar Pichai to block Pentagon access.

title: Musk takes the stand in his OpenAI trial
summary: Elon Musk began testifying in his lawsuit against OpenAI, Sam Altman, and Greg Brockman in a California federal courtroom. Musk is alleging fraud, breach of charitable trust, and unjust enrichment, and is seeking to unwind OpenAI's for-profit restructuring and strip Altman and Brockman of authority. The trial is expected to call major industry witnesses including Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella and former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati.

title: AI regulation is fracturing into sovereign bubbles
summary: A new analysis argues that the long-anticipated convergence of global AI rules is not happening — instead, 2026 is seeing a mosaic of incompatible national regimes. Australia is advancing workplace AI law, Pakistan is restricting government hiring AI, US states are passing a flurry of bills while the White House pushes federal preemption, and the EU can't even agree with itself. For multinationals, the compliance burden is becoming a competitive disadvantage, and shadow AI use is growing as strict internal policies push employees toward personal accounts.

title: The US should build AI standards, not bully AI companies
summary: A sharp analysis argues that the Trump administration's confrontation with Anthropic — threatening to blacklist the company for refusing to remove safety guardrails from Pentagon access — is the wrong play, and risks damaging US credibility in international AI governance. The piece draws a historical parallel to how the US shaped the internet by leading protocol standards through DARPA and NIST, and argues that CAISI's new AI Agent Standards Initiative is the right model to follow. Countries considering adopting US-led AI infrastructure are watching the Anthropic episode carefully.

title: Google's AI Max turns 1, hits Shopping
summary: Google's AI Max for Search campaigns is now expanding to Shopping and travel ad formats, marking one year since its launch as the fastest-growing AI-powered Search ads product. The new AI Brief tool, powered by Gemini, lets advertisers give plain-language instructions about messaging, audience targeting, and what the AI should never say. Text disclaimers are also rolling out to ensure regulated-industry ads stay compliant even when Final URL Expansion is active.

title: SenseNova U1 goes open source
summary: Chinese AI lab SenseNova open-sourced two sizes of its U1 model series, a native multimodal architecture that unifies image understanding and generation without a separate visual encoder or VAE. The NEO-Unify backbone lets the model reason across text and images end-to-end, and early benchmarks put it at open-source state-of-the-art for unified multimodal tasks. A free browser playground and an agent-ready OpenClaw skill interface are available now.

title: Ling-2.6 ships flash and trillion-param flagship
summary: inclusionAI released two models today: Ling-2.6-flash, a 104B-total-parameter MoE model hitting 340 tokens/second on 4x H20 GPUs, and Ling-2.6-1T, a trillion-parameter flagship targeting complex agentic workflows. Both adopt a hybrid MLA plus linear attention architecture to slash VRAM usage and latency on long-context tasks. The 1T model claims open-source SOTA on SWE-bench Verified, BFCL-V4, and AIME26.

title: IBM Granite embeddings get a multilingual R2 upgrade
summary: IBM released Granite-Embedding-97M-Multilingual-R2, swapping XLM-RoBERTa for ModernBERT and extending context from 512 to 32,768 tokens. The model covers 200-plus languages with enhanced support for 52, and a +14.5 point average gain over the prior generation makes it competitive well above its weight class. It ships with ONNX, OpenVINO, and GGUF formats and an Apache 2.0 license.

title: Anthropic eyes $50B round at $900B valuation
summary: Sources say Anthropic is in discussions to raise a new $50 billion funding round that would push its valuation close to $900 billion, a figure that would make it one of the most valuable private companies ever. The potential raise comes as Microsoft deepens its OpenAI partnership and as demand for frontier AI compute continues to accelerate. No deal has been confirmed.

title: Legora hits $5.6B backed by Nvidia
summary: Swedish legal AI startup Legora extended its Series D to $600M total after crossing $100M ARR, with Nvidia's NVentures and Atlassian joining the cap table in a $50M extension round. The new valuation puts Legora at $5.6B, closing the gap somewhat on rival Harvey's $11B. The Nvidia backing marks the chipmaker's first reported investment in the legal AI sector.

title: Netomi pulls $110M with Accenture and Adobe
summary: Customer service AI startup Netomi closed a $110M round led by Accenture Ventures, with Adobe Ventures, Silver Lake Waterman, and WndrCo also participating, and DreamWorks co-founder Jeffrey Katzenberg joining its board. The raise comes as rivals Sierra ($10B valuation) and Decagon ($4.5B) set a high bar for the enterprise customer service AI market. Alongside the investment, Accenture signed a global alliance to bring Netomi to its Fortune 100 client base.

title: 137 Ventures raises $700M for growth-stage bets
summary: SpaceX-backer 137 Ventures announced over $700M across two new growth-stage funds focused on defense, AI, and industrial systems. The firm, founded by former Founders Fund investor Justin Fishner-Wolfson, says it deployed more than a billion dollars in the past year alone. The raise signals continued appetite for late-stage private bets outside the traditional mega-round cycle.

title: Cresta crosses $100M ARR quietly
summary: Contact-center AI startup Cresta, backed by a16z and Sequoia, confirmed it has surpassed $100M in annual recurring revenue in an exclusive to Axios. The milestone comes without a new funding announcement, setting the company apart from louder competitors in the crowded customer service AI space. Cresta was last valued at $1.6B in 2022.

title: EU AI Act delay deal dies in Brussels
summary: The Digital Omnibus political agreement that would have pushed the EU AI Act's Annex III high-risk deadline from August 2026 to December 2027 collapsed Monday after twelve hours of trilogue negotiations. The next formal session is May 13, and nothing has changed legally, meaning the August deadline remains live for high-risk AI system providers. Founders who were banking on the delay now have no fallback.

title: White House AI framework wants states out of the game
summary: A deep dive into the White House's March 2026 National Policy Framework for AI reveals its most controversial feature: broad federal preemption of state AI laws covering development, liability, and AI-enabled activities. The framework rejects a centralized AI regulator and defers copyright questions to the courts, leaving major gaps. Legal experts say no court has yet struck down any state AI law, and the practical advice is to comply with the strictest applicable state standard today.

title: State AI laws pile up faster than federal clarity
summary: New York's RAISE Act for frontier AI models is now finalized and takes effect January 1, 2027, while Washington State enacted a first-of-its-kind Chatbot Disclosure Act requiring companion AI operators to identify themselves. California's metadata labeling law kicks in August 2, and the DOJ AI Litigation Task Force made its first move April 24 by joining xAI's lawsuit against Colorado's SB 205. With no federal preemption law passed and no court ruling yet, businesses face a patchwork of deadlines across dozens of states.

title: Supreme Court gives AI a liability shield boost
summary: A recent Supreme Court ruling limiting copyright contributory liability for Cox Communications has broader implications for AI companies, according to a new legal analysis. The Court clarified that platforms are not liable for user-driven infringement unless they intentionally encouraged it, a standard that could protect AI providers from third-party misuse claims. Legal experts say the ruling aligns with the White House framework's push to limit AI developer liability.

title: GPT-5.5 lands for paid users
summary: OpenAI began rolling out GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Thinking to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise ChatGPT subscribers today. The new Thinking variant is pitched as OpenAI's most capable reasoning model for real-world hard tasks, handling multi-step research, spreadsheet editing, and complex code. API access is coming soon, pending additional safety reviews.

title: FLUX.2 family goes wide on Replicate
summary: Black Forest Labs launched its full FLUX.2 lineup on Replicate, ranging from the ultra-high-fidelity FLUX.2 Max — with hex color control and 8-reference-image support — down to the 4B-parameter FLUX.2 Klein, which generates images in under a second on consumer hardware. The suite covers product photography, text rendering, inpainting, depth-based retexturing, and edge-preserving style transfer. It's the most comprehensive image API refresh the platform has seen in months.

title: ChatGPT comes to Excel and Google Sheets
summary: OpenAI launched a beta of ChatGPT for Excel and Google Sheets, letting Business, Enterprise, Edu, and paid consumer users summon an AI sidebar directly inside their spreadsheets. It can build trackers and budgets from scratch, repair broken formulas, run scenario analyses, and summarize unfamiliar models. Skills — reusable playbooks for specific workflows — are already included for financial modeling and corporate finance formatting.

title: Anthropic's Mythos raises the security stakes
summary: Anthropic quietly released a model called Mythos with unusually strong software vulnerability detection capabilities, and the reaction has been swift. Bloomberg called it globally alarming, while US senators and defense officials urged the Pentagon to deploy it for cybersecurity gap analysis. The release is complicating Anthropic's already fraught relationship with the federal government.

title: Anthropic at $900B — and the clock is ticking
summary: Anthropic is asking investors to submit allocations within 48 hours for what could become a $50 billion fundraise at a valuation approaching $900 billion. Multiple pre-emptive offers have landed in the $850B–$900B range, sources told TechCrunch, making it one of the largest private funding rounds ever attempted. The urgency signals intense competition among investors to get in before terms harden.

title: DeepMind's David Silver raises $1.1B before anyone knew his startup existed
summary: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded just months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver — the architect of AlphaGo — has closed a $1.1 billion round at a $5.1 billion valuation. The lab is pursuing AI that learns without human-labeled data, a research direction Silver championed at DeepMind. The raise is a striking vote of confidence in a founder with deep credibility but zero product yet.

title: Legal AI war: Legora hits $5.6B as it battles Harvey
summary: Legora, a legal AI startup, has reached a $5.6 billion valuation and is locked in an escalating rivalry with Harvey — complete with dueling ad campaigns and head-to-head market expansion. Both companies have raised massive sums in quick succession and are now pushing into each other's core customer bases. The legal AI sector is shaping up as one of the most competitive enterprise software fights of 2026.

title: Anduril nears $60B as Founders Fund writes a big check
summary: Andreessen Horowitz and Thrive Capital are co-leading a roughly $4 billion round in defense tech darling Anduril at a $60 billion valuation, but Founders Fund is emerging as a notable participant with a larger-than-expected allocation. The round cements Anduril's position as the dominant private defense-tech company amid surging government AI and autonomy spending.

title: Parag Agrawal's Parallel Web hits $2B after back-to-back $100M raises
summary: Parallel Web Systems, the AI agent-tooling startup founded by former Twitter CEO Parag Agrawal, has raised another $100 million led by Sequoia, reaching a $2 billion valuation just five months after its previous $100 million round. The rapid re-up signals strong investor conviction that enterprise agent infrastructure is a winner-take-most market.

title: Musk testifies: OpenAI was my idea and they looted it
summary: Elon Musk took the stand in an Oakland courtroom in his lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming the company was originally his concept and that executives later 'looted' its nonprofit mission. The trial, which could force OpenAI to restructure or undo its for-profit transition, is also surfacing embarrassing details — including xAI's admission that it trained Grok on OpenAI model outputs. Legal observers say a loss for OpenAI could effectively eliminate it in its current form.

title: DOJ sides with xAI against Colorado's AI Act
summary: The Department of Justice filed a joint motion with xAI to stay enforcement of Colorado's SB 24-205, an algorithmic discrimination law, marking the first time the federal government has intervened to challenge a state AI law. A federal magistrate judge granted the stay, and the DOJ's move is being read as a broad signal that the Trump administration will use federal power to preempt state-level AI regulation it views as innovation-hostile.

title: Google's Gemini clears classified networks; 600 employees protest
summary: Google amended its $200 million Pentagon contract to allow Gemini AI models to operate on classified government networks for 'any lawful governmental purpose,' following similar deals OpenAI and xAI struck with the Defense Department in March. More than 600 Google AI and Cloud employees signed a letter urging CEO Sundar Pichai to reject the move, warning it could enable lethal autonomous weapons and surveillance the company cannot oversee.

title: Massachusetts court: Meta faces Instagram addiction suit despite Section 230
summary: The Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court ruled unanimously that Meta must face a state lawsuit alleging it deliberately designed Instagram to addict children, becoming the first state high court to hold that Section 230 does not shield platforms from design-based liability claims. Attorney General Andrea Joy Campbell's suit argues the harm stems from how Meta built the platform, not from user-posted content — a distinction that could open the door to similar suits nationwide.

title: OpenAI sued over Canada mass shooting suspect's ChatGPT use
summary: Seven families filed a lawsuit against OpenAI alleging that a mass shooting suspect's extensive use of ChatGPT contributed to the attack in Canada, making it one of the first major tort cases directly linking a generative AI product to real-world violence. The suit is likely to become a landmark test of AI company liability and could shape how platforms design safety guardrails going forward.

title: NVIDIA unleashes Nemotron 3 Nano on Hugging Face
summary: NVIDIA published a sweeping collection of open models on Hugging Face, with Nemotron 3 Nano leading the charge — a hybrid Mamba-2 + Transformer MoE architecture with 30B total parameters (3B active), a 1M-token context window, and reasoning modes you can toggle on or off. The model runs via vLLM and SGLang and is explicitly designed for on-device agentic tasks, delivering up to 4x faster inference than its predecessor. NVIDIA also dropped Cosmos Predict 2.5 for physics-aware video generation and a Nano VL vision-language model trained on 39.5M samples.

title: FLUX.2 family lands on Replicate with sub-second tiers
summary: Black Forest Labs' FLUX.2 lineup is now fully available via Replicate's production API, spanning FLUX.2 Max for high-fidelity product photography with up to 8 reference images, FLUX.2 Pro for structured JSON-prompted generation, and FLUX.2 Klein — a 4B distilled model capable of sub-second inference for real-time apps. The family supports hex color codes for brand consistency and multi-reference character continuity across large batches. It's the most complete image-generation suite Replicate has offered under one roof.

title: Kling Video 3.0 Omni arrives with native audio and multi-shot control
summary: Kling Video 3.0 Omni is now live on Replicate, unifying text-to-video generation with reference image support, in-video editing, native audio tracks, and multi-shot scene control in a single model. The standard Kling 3.0 produces cinematic clips up to 15 seconds with improved consistency. Both models are available via production-ready APIs at launch.

title: Microsoft embeds a legal agent directly into Word
summary: Microsoft is rolling out a dedicated legal AI agent inside Word, aimed at legal teams who want to draft, review, and annotate documents without leaving the editor. The move continues Microsoft's push to embed vertical-specific Copilot agents across its Office suite rather than keeping AI as a general-purpose overlay. No pricing details have been confirmed, but enterprise licensing is expected.

title: Anthropic closing in on a $900B+ valuation round
summary: Sources tell TechCrunch that Anthropic could close a new $50 billion funding round within two weeks, pushing its valuation above $900 billion — a figure that would make it one of the most valuable private companies ever. The round follows rapid enterprise uptake and the high-profile restricted rollout of its Claude Mythos cybersecurity model. White House AI advisor David Sacks acknowledged the Mythos delay made sense from a security standpoint, hinting at growing government interest in Anthropic's work.

title: Meta acquires humanoid robotics startup Assured Robot Intelligence
summary: Meta has bought ARI, a humanoid robotics startup, for an undisclosed sum, folding its team and co-founders into the company's Superintelligence Labs research division. The deal is part of a broader industry sprint into humanoid robots, with analysts projecting the market anywhere from $38 billion by 2035 to $5 trillion by 2050. ARI had raised only a seed round from AI-focused firm Aix Ventures before the acquisition.

title: Anduril valued at $60B as a16z leads $4B round
summary: Andreessen Horowitz and Thrive Capital are leading a roughly $4 billion investment in defense tech firm Anduril at a $60 billion valuation, according to The Information. The raise underscores surging investor appetite for AI-powered defense infrastructure as government AI contracts multiply. The deal is one of the largest single rounds in the defense-tech category to date.

title: Skio sells for $105M after raising only $8M — a rare efficient exit
summary: Y Combinator alum Skio has been acquired for $105 million in cash, a striking return given the company raised just $8 million total, its founder confirmed. The deal stands out in a funding climate where many well-capitalized startups struggle to exit at multiples above their last round. Skio built subscription commerce infrastructure primarily for Shopify brands.

title: Legal AI startup Legora hits $5.6B valuation
summary: Legora has reached a $5.6 billion valuation following its latest funding round, putting it squarely in competition with Harvey in the fast-moving legal AI space. The two companies are now the leading contenders in a sector where law firms are under pressure to adopt AI or fall behind. The rivalry is intensifying as both pursue enterprise contracts with large law firms and in-house legal teams.

title: Musk's OpenAI testimony backfires badly
summary: Elon Musk's week on the stand in his lawsuit against OpenAI did not go as planned — reports from The Vergecast describe him arguing with his own lawyers, contradicting earlier statements, and failing to land clean punches with the jury. The case centers on OpenAI's nonprofit origins and Musk's claim that the company betrayed its founding mission. Legal observers say if Musk cannot make his version of events stick in court, the fight shifts back toward the paper trail and corporate history — territory that may favor OpenAI.

title: DOJ backs xAI in fight to block Colorado's AI Act
summary: The Department of Justice has filed a motion to intervene in xAI's lawsuit against Colorado's Attorney General, supporting the company's argument that the state's AI anti-discrimination law violates the Constitution's equal protection clause. Colorado's AG agreed to a temporary stay on enforcement, prompting the CDT to blast the move. Andreessen Horowitz partners have separately published a proposal for courts to use the Commerce Clause to review state AI laws — a sign the industry is building a coordinated legal strategy against state-level regulation.

title: Dark-money campaign caught paying influencers to hype American AI
summary: Wired reports that a group called Build American AI, tied to a super PAC called Leading the Future backed by tech figures, is paying content creators thousands of dollars per video to promote US AI development and frame China as a threat. The campaign ran in phases, starting with lifestyle creators before escalating to more explicit policy messaging. OpenAI and Palantir both denied corporate involvement, but the operation raises hard questions about undisclosed influence in AI policy debates playing out on social media.

title: Pentagon locks in AI deals — but cuts out Anthropic
summary: The Defense Department has formalized classified AI contracts with OpenAI, Palantir, and xAI for lawful operational use in sensitive settings, while explicitly designating Anthropic a supply chain risk and excluding it from federal work. Anthropic, which had refused to loosen red lines around mass surveillance and autonomous weapons, won a temporary injunction after its products were banned. The standoff is becoming a test case for how national security agencies will choose AI vendors going forward.

title: South Korea targets the full AI content chain with new labeling rules
summary: South Korea is finalizing amendments that would require clear labels on AI-generated photos and videos, expand enforcement powers to block harmful synthetic content faster, and impose obligations not just on AI developers but on uploaders, distributors, and platform operators. The rules are aimed at fake celebrity endorsements and manipulated personas used in advertising. For global entertainment and ad-tech companies working with Korean IP, the shift could require redesigned disclosure workflows and moderation systems.
